git.title: Install Git on macOS
git.summary: Git is needed to clone or update Z3R. Use whichever install route you already trust on your Mac.

steps.title: Check for Apple command line tools
steps.text: Open Terminal and run `git --version`. macOS may prompt you to install Apple's command line developer tools.

steps.title: Use Homebrew if you prefer it
steps.text: If Homebrew is already installed, run `brew install git`, then reopen the launcher and run checks again.

python.title: Install Python on macOS
python.summary: Python is needed for the virtual environment and asset extraction tools.

steps.title: Install Python 3
steps.text: Install Python 3 from python.org or through Homebrew with `brew install python`.

steps.title: Confirm the command works
steps.text: Open a new Terminal window and run `python3 --version`, then return to the launcher and run checks again.

make.title: Install Make on macOS
make.summary: Make drives the macOS build path for Z3R.

steps.title: Install Apple command line tools
steps.text: Open Terminal and run `xcode-select --install`, then follow the Apple installer prompts.

steps.title: Recheck Make
steps.text: After installation, run `make --version` or return to the launcher and press Run Checks.

c-compiler.title: Install a C compiler on macOS
c-compiler.summary: Z3R needs clang or another C compiler to build the native executable.

steps.title: Install Apple command line tools
steps.text: Open Terminal and run `xcode-select --install`. Apple's tools include clang and the cc compatibility command.

steps.title: Recheck the compiler
steps.text: After installation, run `clang --version` or return to the launcher and press Run Checks.

sdl2-dev.title: Install SDL2 development files on macOS
sdl2-dev.summary: The Makefile uses SDL2 compiler flags while building Z3R.

steps.title: Install SDL2
steps.text: If Homebrew is already installed, run `brew install sdl2`.

steps.title: Confirm sdl2-config
steps.text: Open a new Terminal window and run `sdl2-config --version`, then run launcher checks again.

git.title: Install Git on Windows
git.summary: Git is needed to clone or update Z3R from GitHub.

steps.title: Download Git for Windows
steps.text: Use the official Git for Windows installer. When the installer asks how Git should be used from the command line, keep the option that adds Git to PATH.

steps.title: Confirm Git is on PATH
steps.text: Open a new PowerShell window and run `git --version`. If Windows cannot find Git, restart Windows or add Git's `cmd` folder to PATH from Settings > System > About > Advanced system settings > Environment Variables.

steps.title: Restart the launcher
steps.text: After installation, close and reopen the launcher so Git is available on PATH, then run checks again.

python.title: Install Python on Windows
python.summary: Python is needed for the virtual environment and asset extraction tools.

steps.title: Download Python
steps.text: Install Python 3 from python.org. On the first installer screen, enable the `Add python.exe to PATH` checkbox before pressing Install Now.

steps.title: Confirm Python is on PATH
steps.text: Open a new PowerShell window and run `py --version` and `python --version`. If neither works, add Python from Settings > System > About > Advanced system settings > Environment Variables > Path.

steps.title: Confirm the launcher can see Python
steps.text: Open a new PowerShell window and run `py --version`, then return to the launcher and run checks again.

msbuild.title: Install MSBuild on Windows
msbuild.summary: MSBuild is required for the Visual Studio build route.

steps.title: Install Build Tools for Visual Studio
steps.text: Download Build Tools for Visual Studio and select the Desktop development with C++ workload.

steps.title: Make MSBuild available
steps.text: The Visual Studio installer usually exposes MSBuild through Developer PowerShell. If the launcher still cannot find `msbuild`, add the Visual Studio MSBuild `Bin` folder to PATH or start the launcher from Developer PowerShell.

steps.title: Reopen the launcher
steps.text: After installation finishes, reopen the launcher or run it from a Developer PowerShell, then run checks again.

powershell.title: Enable PowerShell on Windows
powershell.summary: PowerShell is used by Windows setup commands and should normally be present on supported Windows installs.

steps.title: Open Windows features or install PowerShell
steps.text: Use the Microsoft guidance to restore Windows PowerShell or install the current PowerShell package.

steps.title: Confirm PowerShell is on PATH
steps.text: Open Command Prompt and run `powershell -NoProfile -Command "$PSVersionTable.PSVersion"`. If it is not found, add the PowerShell install folder to PATH from Environment Variables.

steps.title: Run checks again
steps.text: After PowerShell opens from the Start menu, return to the launcher and run checks again.

tcc.title: Add TCC to the Z3R folder
tcc.summary: TCC is only needed for the lightweight Windows TCC build route.

steps.title: Download the Windows ZIP
steps.text: Open the TCC release page and download the Windows ZIP archive. Extract it, then find `tcc.exe` and the included DLL files inside the extracted folder.

steps.title: Place TCC under third_party
steps.text: Copy the extracted TCC files so `tcc.exe` is at `{projectPath}\third_party\tcc\tcc.exe`. Keep the DLL files from the ZIP in that same TCC folder.

steps.title: Run checks again
steps.text: After the file is in place, return to the launcher and press Run Checks.

sdl2.title: Add SDL2 to the Z3R folder
sdl2.summary: SDL2.dll is required by the Windows TCC route and by the built game executable.

steps.title: Download SDL2 development files
steps.text: Download the SDL2 2.30.11 development package for Visual C++ from the linked release page.

steps.title: Place the DLL where Z3R expects it
steps.text: Put `SDL2.dll` at `{projectPath}\third_party\SDL2-2.26.3\lib\x64\SDL2.dll`.

git.title: Install Git on Linux
git.summary: Git is needed to clone or update Z3R from GitHub.

steps.title: Use your distribution package manager
steps.text: Install the package named `git` with your distro's normal package manager.

steps.title: Run checks again
steps.text: Open a new terminal, confirm `git --version`, then return to the launcher.

python.title: Install Python on Linux
python.summary: Python is needed for the virtual environment and asset extraction tools.

steps.title: Install Python 3
steps.text: Install your distro's Python 3 package.

steps.title: Install venv support on Debian/Ubuntu
steps.text: Run `sudo apt-get install python3-venv`, or install the versioned package Python reports.

steps.title: Confirm Python works
steps.text: Run `python3 --version`, then return to the launcher and run checks again.

make.title: Install Make on Linux
make.summary: Make drives the Linux build path for Z3R.

steps.title: Install build tools
steps.text: Install your distro's build-essential or base-devel package group, which includes Make.

steps.title: Run checks again
steps.text: Confirm `make --version`, then return to the launcher.

c-compiler.title: Install a C compiler on Linux
c-compiler.summary: Z3R needs gcc or clang to build the native executable. Some SteamOS/Steam Deck environments include SDL2 and Make but do not provide a cc compiler command.

steps.title: Install build tools
steps.text: On Debian/Ubuntu or apt-based systems, install `build-essential`. On Arch-based systems, install a compiler package such as `gcc` or the `base-devel` group.

steps.title: Packaged Linux note
steps.text: AppImage and Flatpak launcher releases download the matching Z3R executable instead of compiling it locally. Source runs and native Linux builds still need gcc or clang from the system.

steps.title: Run checks again
steps.text: Confirm `gcc --version` or `clang --version`, then return to the launcher.

sdl2-dev.title: Install SDL2 development files on Linux
sdl2-dev.summary: The Makefile uses SDL2 compiler and linker flags while building Z3R.

steps.title: Install SDL2 development package
steps.text: On Debian/Ubuntu or apt-based Unix systems, run `sudo apt-get install libsdl2-dev`.

steps.title: Other Linux distributions
steps.text: Use your distro's SDL2 development package, usually `libsdl2-dev` or `sdl2-devel`.

steps.title: Confirm sdl2-config
steps.text: Run `sdl2-config --version`, then return to the launcher and run checks again.
